summary: When MSAA 3D is enabled in project settings, CompositorEffects in Compositor are only applied when EffectCallbackType = Post Transparent.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot v4.4.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6614) - AMD Ryzen 9 3900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

When MSAA 3D is set to anything other than disabled. In a 3D scene, any CompositorEffects inside a Compositor inside a WorldEnvironment Node will only work when its Effect Callback Type is set to Post Transparent.

Post Transparent:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4ebaa7cf-80a3-4153-aff7-b14c4cc95c4f)

Pre Transparent:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/33eab78c-89e9-43c0-9cc4-1b23470f358e)

All objects except the sphere are opaque, and so only the sphere should be unaffected by the shader.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create new 3D project.
2. Populate scene with some shapes to observe your shader on, plus a camera of course.
3. Create a glsl file for a compute shader (inverting colors is an easy shader).
4. Create gdscript or C# file extending Compositor Effect and set up API calls.
5. Set up a WorldEnvironment node with an environment and Compositor.
6. Attached your created CompositorEffect to the Compositor and change its EffectCallbackType to anything but Post Transparent.
7. Set MSAA 3D to anything but disabled.
8. Run project.
9. The shader you wrote should only work when the compositor effect it's attached to is Post Transparent.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[CompositorEffectBug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20406235/CompositorEffectBug.zip)

summary: BoneAttachment should capture the results from the AnimationMixer before IK processing

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.4.1

### System information

Arch Linux, 16GB RAM, 10th gen I5 intel CPU, AMD graphics 70XX generation

### Issue description

The Issue:
I'm working on a shooting game and I depend on the IK nodes to attach the character's hands to the guns in the editor everything looks fine but in gameplay the IK nodes update really slow resulting in the hands' movement lagging behind the gun, I experimented with physics and idle callback modes and I found if the the animations are tied to the physics and the physics FPS is double that of the graphics FPS the issue goes away but for my project I need the physics capped at 60.

I suspect the IK is hardcoded to have a single frame delay behind every update.

The Solution:
add an option to make the IK node update perfectly every single graphics frame.

### Steps to reproduce

1) add bone attachment node to gun(the gun's rig is apart of the player's rig too)
2) add IK node to same rig and configure it to attach the hand to said bone attachment node beginning with the shoulder as the root bone of the IK chain.
3) run game

running in the editor looks correct but in gameplay there is a delay in the IK node movement.

I can't share the project I'm working on.

summary: Android: Add export option for custom theme attributes

description.text:
- Regenerate the `GodotAppMainTheme` and `GodotAppSplashTheme` during Android export. Any manual changes to this style will be cleared and replaced with default theme attributes.

- Adds a new export option `gradle_build/custom_theme_attributes` for injecting custom theme attributes directly via the export window, avoiding the need to manually modify themes.xml.

It helps to avoid the issue I metioned in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/106709#discussion_r2103088921.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9326e82d-3843-4a15-ab4e-5bc29a212f5f)
According to the attributes shown in the image:
- The first pair adds `<item name="android:windowIsTranslucent">true</item>` to the GodotAppMainTheme.
- In the second pair key is prefixed with `[splash]`, so it adds the attribute to the GodotAppSplashTheme.

summary: In GDScript, assigning -0.0 to a float variable causes the next float variable of 0.0 to incorrectly initialize as -0.0

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproduced in v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]
Reproduces in an empty project with no plugins and a single script, see below.

### System information

Windows 10 - v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### Issue description

In GDScript, assigning a value of -0.0 to a float var will cause the next float var assigned with 0.0 to also get a value of -0.0.

For example:
```
var a: float = -0.0
var b: float = 0.0
print("%.1f" % a) # -0.0
print("%.1f" % b) # -0.0, expected 0.0
```

The same issue happens in reverse:
```
var a: float = 0.0
var b: float =- 0.0
print("%.1f" % a) # 0.0
print("%.1f" % b) # 0.0, expected -0.0
```

Note that `.format()` hides the issue, because it always displays the value as 0.0, even when the float is -0.0.

I had originally thought this was an issue with string formatting.
~~1. This only occurs if the value is cached as a `float var`, like in the examples. Just passing 0.0 or -0.0 directly in (`"%.1f" % -0.0`) works as expected.
2. If we do 3+ calls in a row, all the subsequent ones match the sign of the first regardless of the expected sign.
3. Calling the next format _from a different function_ resets us back to the correct behavior, like the first format of each of the examples above. However, entering a scope like an `if` does not reset the behavior.
4. It doesn't matter if the format string is `"%.1f"` or `"%f"` with respect to the sign. Same issues either way.
5. I fail to repro this when using .format, because .format does not seem to work the way the [docs](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/scripting/gdscript/gdscript_format_string.html) describe. For example, the docs suggest writing `var string = "I have {} cats.".format([3])`, but for me this just returns "I have {} cats." I may log a separate issue for this, and link it below.~~ 
6. I have attached a test project

### Steps to reproduce

In Godot v4.4.1.stable.official, create a new project. Add a 3D scene. Add a script to the scene with the following:
```
var a: float = -0.0
var b: float = 0.0
print("%.1f" % a) # -0.0
print("%.1f" % b) # -0.0, expected 0.0
```

Alternately, use my minimal reproduction project.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[print-test-2.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20400087/print-test-2.zip)

summary: Deprecate `Control::accept_event`

description.text:
`Control::accept_event` does `get_viewport()->_gui_accept_event()` only if the control is inside the tree. `Viewport::_gui_accept_event` does `set_input_as_handled()` only if the viewport is inside the tree. 

A strange method. Why would it be called outside the tree? Why make a private method in a separate class that only calls two public methods? Why the checks?

The viewport returned by `get_viewport()` in `accept_event` will always be in the tree, meaning `_gui_accept_event` has no purpose beyond calling the `set_input_as_handled`, which could have been in `accept_event` directly.

I also find the existence of `accept_event` itself unnecessary and misleading. It's just `get_viewport()->set_input_as_handled()` except instead of getting an error when it isn't in the tree (`get_viewport()` would be null), it just silently does nothing as if the user hadn't done anything wrong, when doing either outside of the tree should be a clear error on the user's part. 

The user should never call `accept_event()` where they would not call `get_viewport()->set_input_as_handled()`. That is, they should not be calling either outside of an input method. If they _are_ in an input method, there is an event to handle, therefore, there must be a viewport inside the tree that propagated the event, making the checks unnecessary. If the user _really_ wants to accept input events outside of an input method (if they even can), they are free to do their own check if they're in the tree. Otherwise, using `set_input_as_handled` is always the better option.

I have made a slight modification and deprecated `Control::accept_event` in favor of `Viewport::set_input_as_handled`. Since `accept_event` was the only user of the useless `Viewport::_gui_accept_event`, it has been removed.

summary: [WIP Prototype] Add HDR support to tonemappers

description.text:
This prototype demonstrates how tonemappers could behave when HDR output is enabled. Please provide feedback in the comments! You can use Windows Game bar to take HDR screenshots OBS Studio has good support for HDR video recording as well (let me know if you want me to give you OBS configuration details).

This branch builds on top of #94496. Please share any feedback relating to HDR (not tonemappers) on that PR instead! Many of the changes from this PR should be merged in [a separate PR](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/106940), as these changes include adding `white` parameter support to the AgX tonemapper, which is required independently of HDR output support.

This draft PR implements godotengine/godot-proposals#12317, including the addition of two new tonemaping user parameters: `black` and `contrast`, both of which I feel are required for flexible and stable HDR output behaviour. Please review this proposal for rationale.

Importantly, **Flimic and ACES tonemappers cannot support HDR output**. This is because the `white` parameter of these tonemappers was designed exclusively for SDR and this style of white parameter cannot work with HDR and variable Extended Dynamic Range (EDR). This PR demonstrates how they should behave in HDR mode: exactly the same as they behave in SDR mode, limited to [0.0, 1.0] range.

Because Filmic cannot support HDR mode, I have introduced a new tonemapper that is HDR-compatible to replace it: the **Adjustable** tonemapper. Unlike AgX, this tonemapper does not desaturate colours to white as they become very bright and applies the tone curve directly in the linear sRGB working colour space, just like Filmic does. It's configuration parameters match AgX and it can be configured to appear similar to Filmic in SDR.

[Check out videos of how this new "Adjustable" tonemapping curve works on my blog post.](https://allenwp.com/blog/2025/05/29/allenwp-tonemapping-curve/)

On a related note, the new configuration parameters of AgX (specifically `contrast`) can be used to make it appear similar to ACES, but with correct HDR output support.

## Usage

[Download windows-editor Artifact from the Checks page of my branch.](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/106696/checks)

The basics of tonemapping in Godot is [covered in the docs](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/3d/environment_and_post_processing.html#tonemap). In this PR I've updated the in-engine docs to have some more detail.

HDR output is covered in detail in the original PR #94496, but here's a quick summary:

1. Enable 2D HDR: `rendering/viewport/hdr_2d`
2. Enable HDR output: `display/window/hdr/enabled`

### To control HDR brightness settings:

**Option 1:** Simply change the SDR content brightness in Windows (you might need to move or resize the Godot window to force it to refresh):
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f44ce763-f53b-4a0e-8701-4f28accfaf71)

**Option 2:** Disable `display/window/hdr/use_screen_luminance` and manually set the Reference Luminance (this is equivalent to SDR content brightness) and Max Luminance.

**Note:** Reducing the max luminance with Option 2 may be necessary to circumvent the built-in tonemapping of your display.

## Performance

Performance of the AgX tonemapper is better than the current implementation in `master`. I wasn't able to get as good of performance as my best attempt using Timothy Lottes' curve in #102435, but the stability and predictability of the curve in this PR across all of variable EDR is a reasonable trade off that gives a significantly better user experience when targeting both SDR and HDR.

A number of additional parameters are now passed from the CPU to the GPU, increasing the number of bytes passed into the tonemapper. This has a minor impact on performance (barely measurable on my NVIDIA 980 Ti for a 4K window). Unfortunately, this affects all tonemappers... which leads me to my next point:

Adding another tonemapper to Godot, as it is currently implemented, causes a notable performance degradation to **all** tonemappers. This happened in Godot 4.4 when AgX was added to Godot, and it will happen again when the new Adjustable tonemapper is added. Work needs to be done to address this at a structural level.

The `black` parameter makes ALL tonemapping notably slower. I haven't looked into how this can be optimized yet; this PR simply demonstrates how it should look when it is correctly implemented.

## Limitations

- Windows only
- Currently only works with Mobile and Forward+ rendering methods.
- Optimization work is not complete: It's possible these changes cause a minor performance regression and performance of the new tonemappers is not representative of their final optimized form.

## Known Issues

- My intent is for Reinhard to behave differently than SDR when `white` is less than 1.0 and HDR has been enabled. The code currently checks to see if `max_value != 1.0` instead of checking if HDR is enabled, but this is incorrect because `max_value` can equal `1.0` when HDR is enabled and `reference_luminance == max_luminance`.

summary: get_datetime_dict_from_unix_time is linear-time: can cause freeze lasting several minutes if called on a long-ago or far-in-the-future timestamp

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.4.1 - code in `time.cpp` responsible has been there for 4 years so very likely this bug has existed for that long

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Single-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (Intel Corporation; 32.0.101.6737) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-13700H (20 threads)

### Issue description

`Time.get_datetime_dict_from_unix_time` uses a constant time algorithm (in `core/os/time.cpp`) wrt the timestamp to determine year, which means for very negative or very positive values (uncommon but possible, particularly if using minimum signed int as a default to represent "never" or if receiving timestamps from outside Godot) a long freeze may be observed while the algorithm iterates over as many years as the timestamp represents relative to 1970. 

### Steps to reproduce

Call `Time.get_datetime_dict_from_unix_time` on a very negative value: e.g. `Time.get_datetime_dict_from_unix_time(-9223372036854775807)` causes a ~2 minute freeze, `Time.get_datetime_dict_from_unix_time(-922337203685477)` causes a ~120ms freeze

To use the project, simply run the default scene and click the button, observe the freeze.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[get_datetime_dict_from_unix_time-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20375642/get_datetime_dict_from_unix_time-mrp.zip)

summary: Code completion sorting ranks better matches lower

description.text: ### Tested versions 4.3, 4.4, has been present since 4.0 at least partially ### System information x ### Issue description There is something off and illogical with how code completions are sorted. I've personally noticed this mainly with completion for `print()`, just because it's used often and annoys me every time, but of course there are also in many other situations where it occurs. When writing `pri`, i would expect `print()` to be the first suggestion. it is the shortest and closest match to what i have written so far. but it is the fourth option in the list. this doesn't even change when i've written the full word and if i were to press tab now, i'd get a totally different function than the exact match i just typed. This is likely just from "alphabetical" sorting, where `_` just has a higher score than any other character. but it shouldn't - "no character" should always rank higher. like it does in other editors, like vscode ![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4805046a-32a0-48d7-ba6b-35033129f4ef) further, since a few updates ago we can now type parts of words, or initials to get a better matching completion quicker. this is very nice. but it's still unpredictable. if i were to type `prr`, for **pr**int_**r**ich() i get the following matches with the second character in the second word for some reason? print_rich nowhere to be seen. let's add one more letter, `prri` again, why does the 2nd and 3rd character in the second word rank higher than the 1st and 2nd in the second word. even the 2nd and 3rd in the third word rank higher. There are a few open completion issues and proposals that would improve the completion experience overall, but don't mention this core issue. - https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/discussions/9178 - https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/10665 - https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11079 Why this is a bug issue and not a proposal: The behaviour (especially the 2nd character situation) feels broken when using autocompletion and is unexpected both logically and compared to other code editors. Small note: this has been bugging me since 4.0, mostly for print(), but never enough to bring it up. i've recently talked with a few other users who had the same experience, finding it illogical and unexpected, that prompted me to open an issue ### Steps to reproduce typing any keyword with autocompletion that has other similar keywords ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) empty project

summary: Relax the requirements for making `EditorImportPlugin`

description.text:
The example `EditorImportPlugin` code in the class reference does not work because it didn't override methods like `_get_priority()`, `_get_import_order()`, and `_get_option_visibility()`.

- `_get_priority()` and `_get_import_order()`: These two methods have documented default value. This PR allows not overridding these two methods. Default values mentioned in the documentation will be used in this case.
- `_get_option_visibility()`: This method is for advanced usage and should provide a default implementation that always returns true, making all options visible.

Other changes:

- `_get_preset_count()`: Defaults to zero when not overridden.
- `_get_preset_name()`: When not overridden and the preset count is positive, the editor errors and uses an empty string as the preset name, making the dropdown menu look broken. This PR makes it return the preset index as the error return value.
- Fixed broken preset menu when `_get_preset_count()` mistakenly returns a negative value.

summary: Change editor debugger error list jump action to double-click to avoid accidental trigger

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html
-->
Currently, the behavior of jumping to the error location is bind to the `Tree::item_selected` signal, which results in jumping to the error location in the editor with only one click on the error item. It is easy to accidental trigger (e.g. when you try to click the expand button to the left of the error item). And right-clicking can also trigger this signal, making it impossible to access the right-click menu without jumping to the error location. These issues are especially noticeable when using external text editors and harm the user experience
This pr binds the behavior of jumping to the error location to the `Tree::item_activate` signal, so that only double-clicking on the error item or pressing enter after selecting it will jump to the error location

summary: Fix finished signal on GPU particles

description.text:
Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/93991

Fixes two issues:
1. `finished` signal is now emitted when continuously emitting `GPUParticles2D` (or 3D) is stopped by setting `one_shot` to true. 
    - Previously `finished` signal was not emitted and it was extremely difficult to work with particles. I have a lot of use cases where I need to do something when the particles are stopped.
    - There was a workaround which at least sometimes worked. Calling `gpu_particles_2d.restart()` in `_ready()` function allowed `finished` signal to emit.

So this kind of code works now:
```gdscript
func _on_button_pressed() -> void:
	gpu_particles_2d.one_shot = true
	gpu_particles_2d.finished.connect(func(): prints("FINISHED!!!!!"))
```

2. Setting `one_shot` to `false` when it already is `false` no longer resets the particle system. 

I tested master's CPUParticles2D and that doesn't have same problem.

summary: Area3D does not handle triangle-based shapes correctly when using Jolt Physics

description.text:
### Tested versions

Issue with Godot v4.4, 4.4.1, and 4.5.dev (with built-in Jolt)
Regression from 4.3 (with Jolt addon)

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Windows 10.0.19044 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7640) - AMD Ryzen 9 5950X 16-Core Processor (32 Threads)

### Issue description

(This issue is a follow-up to #101721)

There exists a regression in how Jolt the addon detected ConcavePolygonShape3D in 4.3, versus how Jolt detects it since being embedded into core.

I have two scenes that work as parametrized stair and ladder generators. They both use most of the same code, with the stairs generated as arranged StaticBody3Ds (with a BoxShape), and ladders generated as arranged CSGCylinders with collision enabled (therefore a ConcavePolygonShape3D). summary: Fix error when closing all scene tabs on the last tab

description.text:
To reproduce the error, open two scenes and right click on the last scene tab, choose "Close All Tabs".

The scenes will close successfully, but an error will be printed:

```
ERROR: Index p_idx = 1 is out of bounds (edited_scene.size() = 1).
   at: get_scene_path (editor/editor_data.cpp:893)
```

This is because this context menu has two `id_pressed` handlers, one forwarding to `EditorNode`'s scene menu, the other is for custom menu items added by plugins:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/be3ecaeb3c51433058ad4e96ec892d18f4291efd/editor/gui/editor_scene_tabs.cpp#L434-L435

The error is caused by the menu item IDs in this context menu starting from 3000.

- A very large number was picked so they don't clash with `EditorNode`'s scene menu item IDs.
- However, IDs starting from 2000 are considered custom menu items added by plugins.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/be3ecaeb3c51433058ad4e96ec892d18f4291efd/editor/gui/editor_scene_tabs.h#L49-L55

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/be3ecaeb3c51433058ad4e96ec892d18f4291efd/editor/plugins/editor_context_menu_plugin.h#L58

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/be3ecaeb3c51433058ad4e96ec892d18f4291efd/editor/gui/editor_scene_tabs.cpp#L223-L227

In addition to being mistakened as a custom menu item, if the built-in menu item causes the current tab to be closed before the second handler `EditorSceneTabs::_custom_menu_option` is called, this error will occur.

This PR changes the starting item ID to 1000 so only one of the two handlers will be called.

summary: macOS: Support vsync when embedding OpenGL processes

description.text:
Closes #106379

OpenGL was not using a swap-chain tied to a display or a CALayer that manages vsync, so it was running at an unbounded frame rate and causing stutter. This PR introduces changes to track the CG display for the window displaying the embedded content, and passes the CG display ID to the embedded process, so that a `CVDisplayLink` can be associated with the correct display. As the window moves from displays, it adjusts its frame rate accordingly. My system, with 120 Hz and 60 Hz displays, the frame rate changes correctly as I move the window on each screen.

### Issue description

When ***AN*** editor feature profile is active, ClassDB will print `ERROR: Cannot get class 'MyCustomResource'` one or multiple times every time a re-import happens for a file that's been imported into a `MyCustomResource` type by an EditorImportPlugin implementation.

The content of the editor feature profile is arbitrary; the error persists even though all Node, Class, and Feature Sets are enabled.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Download the MRP project archive.
2. Open it with the Godot Editor 4.4.1.
3. Check to ensure the `MRP.mrpdoc` is imported correctly.
4. Import the included feature profile in the `Editor/Managed Editor Features`.
5. Reimport the `MRP.mrpdoc` to observe the issue.

Context: The `MRP.mrpdoc` itself is a UTF-8 encoded text file.
